narrative_blocks.header: Both sons became senior Episcopal clergy
narrative_blocks.body: Her elder son Herman Hollerith IV serves as Bishop of the Episcopal Diocese of Southern Virginia. Her younger son Randolph Marshall Hollerith is Dean of Washington National Cathedral.

narrative_blocks.header: Descended from a Virginia Whig Congressman
narrative_blocks.body: Her great-great-grandfather Willoughby Newton II represented Virginia in the 28th Congress. He served the Virginia House of Delegates across four decades and led the Virginia Agricultural Society.

career.organization: Prince George Winyah, Georgetown, South Carolina

career.role: Bishop (10th) of the Episcopal Diocese of Southern Virginia
career.notes: Elected September 27, 2008. Consecrated February 13, 2009 by Presiding Bishop Katharine Jefferts Schori. Retired December 31, 2018. Predecessor: David Conner Bane Jr. Successor: Susan Bunton Haynes. During his tenure: led significant diocesan reorganization; relocated diocesan office; led the diocese's response to the legacies of slavery and racism (Repairers of the Breach Task Force, 2013 service of repentance and apology); supported full inclusion of LGBTQ Christians (Living a Holy Life Task Force). Served on the board of the College for Bishops, House of Bishops Committee on Pastoral Development, Disciplinary Board for Bishops, and board of Berkeley Divinity School (Episcopal seminary at Yale).

narrative_blocks.header: Consecrated by the first woman Presiding Bishop
narrative_blocks.body: Katharine Jefferts Schori, the Episcopal Church’s first female Presiding Bishop, consecrated him in February 2009, and he led Southern Virginia’s diocese for nine years. His younger brother Randolph is Dean of Washington National Cathedral — two Hollerith brothers at once in the church’s senior ranks.

While Holly led the Diocese of Southern Virginia, his younger brother Randolph became Dean of Washington National Cathedral — Hollerith brothers holding two of the Episcopal Church’s most visible posts at once.

cross_connections.display_label: Great-grandson of Herman Hollerith, IBM founder and punch-card inventor
cross_connections.description: Bishop Hollerith's great-grandfather was Herman Hollerith Sr., inventor of the electromechanical punched-card tabulating machine and founder of the Tabulating Machine Company that became IBM. The bishop's first name 'Herman' continues the four-generation family naming pattern (Herman Sr → Herman Jr → Herman III → Herman IV).

residence.notes: Lives on the Cathedral Close at Washington National Cathedral.

education.institution: Berkeley Divinity School at Yale University
education.notes: Met his future wife Melissa here. Berkeley Divinity School is the Episcopal seminary affiliated with Yale Divinity School.

career.notes: Ordained June 2, 1990 by Bishop Peter J. Lee.
Ordained April 16, 1991 by Bishop Peter J. Lee.

career.organization: St. James's Episcopal Church, Richmond, Virginia
career.notes: 16 years as rector before being called to the National Cathedral.

career.organization: Washington National Cathedral / Episcopal Diocese of Washington
career.notes: Named 11th Dean in August 2016. Spearheaded the 5-year Strategic Plan with four core priorities: Welcoming, Deepening, Convening, Serving. Led the 5-year 'A Cathedral for the Future' campaign which raised $185 million. Oversaw the complete restoration of the former College of Preachers as the new Virginia Mae Center for the Cathedral College of Faith & Culture. Completed restoration work from the 2011 earthquake. Renovated the Cathedral's organ. Expanded the endowment. Invested in critical digital infrastructure. During COVID-19 pandemic (closed Cathedral nearly 18 months), led the emergence of a digital Cathedral that doubled the donor base and reached 5+ million people online. Operating surpluses every year since 2017. Led State Funerals for President George H.W. Bush (2018) and Jimmy Carter (2025). Funeral rites for Senators John McCain, Bob Dole, John Warner; Secretaries of State Colin Powell, Madeleine Albright.

awards.name: Member of the Order of St John (MStJ)
Washingtonian to Watch
awards.notes: Named one of 21 Washingtonians to watch in the Washington City Paper's annual People Issue.

narrative_blocks.header: Eleventh dean of Washington National Cathedral
narrative_blocks.body: Randolph 'Randy' Hollerith was named the 11th Dean of Washington National Cathedral in August 2016. During his tenure he led State Funerals for Presidents George H.W. Bush and Jimmy Carter, plus funeral rites for Senators John McCain, Bob Dole, and John Warner, and Secretaries of State Colin Powell and Madeleine Albright.

Hollerith grew up in nearby Alexandria, often visiting the Cathedral as a boy during its construction; his family had longstanding ties to the institution. His great-grandfather Herman Hollerith was the inventor of the punched-card tabulating machine and the founder of the Tabulating Machine Company that became IBM in 1924.

cross_connections.display_label: Great-grandson of Herman Hollerith, IBM founder and punch-card inventor
cross_connections.description: Randolph Hollerith's great-grandfather was Herman Hollerith Sr., inventor of the electromechanical punched-card tabulating machine deployed in the 1890 U.S. Census, and founder of the Tabulating Machine Company that merged in 1911 to form CTR — renamed International Business Machines (IBM) in 1924. The Hollerith family had longstanding ties to the National Cathedral that Randolph would later lead as Dean.

burialCemetery.name: Leeds Episcopal Church Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Leeds Episcopal Church Cemetery — burial site of Herman Hollerith III and his wife Agnes Newton Hollerith.

INST146.name: Johns Hopkins University
INST146.notes:
Founded 1876 Baltimore MD with a $7 million bequest from Quaker entrepreneur Johns Hopkins. The first American research university on the German model — pioneering the graduate doctoral program. Schools of particular tree relevance: School of Medicine (1893, with attached Hospital), Krieger School of Arts and Sciences, Whiting School of Engineering.

HOOKER-TREE CONNECTIONS — 11 across Hooker and Talcott branches, concentrated in medicine and graduate education:

MEDICINE:
- HD04886 Dr. Sarah Hooker Knutti (Hopkins School of Medicine)
- I00334 Doctor James Shepherd (Hopkins Hospital)
- HD1183 Doctor George Burton Gilbert
- I00276 Agnes Boxley Newton Hollerith

BALTIMORE FAMILY CLUSTER (overlaps with Brown Memorial Presbyterian INST147):
- I01138 Bayard Turnbull (Baltimore architect)
- I01141 Jerome Herrick Tyson 'Jerry' Kidder (Baltimore Gas & Electric)

WOOLSEY FAMILY CLUSTER:
- HD1236 Alexander Hamilton 'Sandy' Woods Woolsey
- HD2073 John Mumford Woolsey (Federal Judge — Ulysses obscenity trial 1933)

OTHERS:
- HD1342 Sophia Joanna Hill Stratton
-
- X02091 Jean Alexandra McCain Morgan

Hopkins's Baltimore location made it the natural medical and graduate destination for the Jones-Sparhawk Baltimore family cluster centered around Brown Memorial Presbyterian Church (INST147) — TD0240 Rev. John Sparhawk Jones's founding pastorate 1870+.
